spec_report.summary: PymooLab metric plugin for 'igd_llm': a from-scratch, GitHub-converted implementation of the Inverted Generational Distance (IGD) indicator with a configurable p-norm exponent for an arbitrary number of objectives. IGD is defined as (1/|PF|) * sum_{z in PF} [ min_{s in S} ||z - s||_p ] — i.e., for each reference-front point z, compute the p-norm distance to the nearest approximation-front point s, then average over all |PF| reference points. The plugin exposes `p` (float, default 2.0) as a user-tunable hyperparameter, accepting any p >= 1. Distances per reference point are computed via scipy.spatial or vectorised NumPy broadcasting over the full (|PF| x |S| x n_obj) tensor to avoid Python loops. The final scalar is lower-is-better; zero indicates that every reference point is exactly covered by at least one solution. The implementation is a direct GitHub-converted translation of the classical formula found in jMetal/jMetalPy, pymoo, and the pyMultiobjective indicator family, preserving exact metric semantics and NOT wrapping pymoo's native InvertedGenerationalDistance class.
assumptions: Both the approximation front S (shape: [n_solutions, n_obj]) and the reference front PF (shape: [n_ref, n_obj]) are provided as 2-D NumPy arrays of floats in objective space; no decision-variable coordinates are needed.
PF is a dense, pre-sampled discrete approximation of the true Pareto front supplied externally by the user or benchmark problem; the plugin does not generate PF internally.
p >= 1.0 is enforced to guarantee the p-norm satisfies the triangle inequality; p=2 recovers the classical Euclidean IGD, p=1 recovers Manhattan IGD.
No objective-space normalisation is applied inside the plugin by default; normalisation (e.g., dividing by the nadir–ideal range) is the caller's responsibility.
S must be non-empty (at least one solution) and PF must be non-empty (at least one reference point); violations raise a ValueError at call time.
n_obj (number of objectives) is inferred at runtime from the column dimension of S and PF; both must agree on n_obj.

Objectives are assumed to be minimisation-oriented, consistent with pymoo convention; the formula is direction-agnostic but the semantic meaning (lower IGD = better convergence+diversity) holds only for minimisation.
The metric is a scalar float in [0, +inf); the plugin returns a Python float (or 1-element NumPy scalar), not an array.
scipy >= 1.0 is available in the PymooLab runtime for optional use of scipy.spatial.cdist; fallback to pure NumPy broadcasting is also supported.
invariants: Formula: IGD_p(S, PF) = mean_{z in PF}( min_{s in S} ||z - s||_p ) where ||.||_p is the standard Minkowski p-norm over n_obj dimensions.
The outer aggregation is a simple arithmetic mean over |PF| reference points, NOT a p-th power mean; only the per-point distance uses the p-norm.
For p=2 the result must be numerically identical (to float64 tolerance) to the classical Euclidean IGD used in jMetal, jMetalPy, and the pyMultiobjective IGD indicator.
The minimum over S for each reference point z is a true global minimum, not an approximation; full pairwise distance computation is required.
Result is invariant to permutation of rows in S and permutation of rows in PF.
Result is zero if and only if every z in PF equals at least one s in S (exact coverage).
The plugin does NOT aggregate or average across multiple runs; it computes a single-call deterministic scalar.
The plugin must be lower-is-better; metadata must declare direction='minimize'.
p is a float hyperparameter stored on the metric object and must not be mutated between calls (stateless computation).
limitations: IGD is not Pareto-compliant in general: a set with worse convergence can occasionally yield a better (lower) IGD than a Pareto-superior set if the distribution of reference points is uneven.
Result is sensitive to the density and distribution of the reference front PF; sparse or biased PF sampling can render IGD misleading, especially in many-objective settings.

Computational complexity is O(|PF| * |S| * n_obj) per call; for very large fronts (>10k points) and high n_obj, this can be slow without a KD-tree or FAISS index — the plugin does not implement approximate nearest-neighbor search.
For p -> infinity the p-norm converges to the Chebyshev (L-inf) norm; numerical stability of very large p values is not guaranteed and the plugin should warn or clamp if p > 100.
IGD does not measure spread or distribution quality independently; it conflates convergence and diversity into one scalar.
The plugin has no access to the true analytical Pareto front; PF quality directly limits IGD validity.
For n_obj > 10 (many-objective), IGD values become harder to interpret comparatively across problems with different scales; normalisation should be applied externally.
Unlike IGD+ (Ishibuchi et al., 2015), this IGD variant is not Pareto-compliant and can reward dominated solutions; users needing compliance should use a separate igd_plus_llm plugin.
spec_report.known_family_hint: IGD / Generational Distance family (GD, IGD, GD+, IGD+). Direct academic lineage: Van Veldhuizen & Lamont (1998) for GD; Zhang & Li MOEA/D IEEE TEC 2007 for IGD as commonly cited; Sierra & Coello Coello (2004) for the p-norm generalisation. jMetal InvertedGenerationalDistance.java and jMetalPy quality_indicator.py are the primary open-source reference implementations. pymoo's native `pymoo.indicators.igd.IGD` uses p=2 Euclidean distance and does NOT expose p as a parameter — this plugin fills that gap with a standalone, p-configurable, GitHub-converted implementation.
spec_report.metric_kind_hint: convergence-and-diversity composite quality indicator (reference-front-based, lower-is-better scalar, multi-objective performance metric)
ambiguity_notes: OUTER AGGREGATION FORM — Two conventions exist: (A) arithmetic mean (1/|PF|)*sum d_p(z,S) [standard, used in jMetal, jMetalPy, MOEA/D paper]; (B) p-th root of mean of p-th powers (1/|PF| * sum d(z,S)^p)^(1/p) [occasionally used to match the GD paper notation]. This spec mandates convention (A): plain arithmetic mean of p-norm distances. The LLM agent must implement (A) and document the choice.

P-NORM SCOPE — 'p-norm' is ambiguous: it could mean (i) p-norm used only for the per-point distance ||z-s||_p (this spec), or (ii) p applied also to the outer aggregation. This spec uses p only in the inner per-point Minkowski distance; the outer aggregation is always a plain mean.

NORMALISATION — Some community usages normalise objectives by nadir-ideal range before computing IGD; others do not. This plugin defaults to NO normalisation. An optional `normalize: bool = False` flag may be exposed but must default to False to match the canonical formula.

REFERENCE FRONT SOURCE — PymooLab may pass PF from a benchmark problem's `.pareto_front()` method or from a user-supplied CSV; the plugin must accept any 2-D float array and must not assume PF originates from a specific problem.

PYMOO NATIVE CLASS — pymoo has `pymoo.indicators.igd.IGD` but it hardcodes Euclidean (p=2) and uses `scipy.spatial.distance.cdist` internally; the open GitHub issue #153 confirms p is not user-adjustable. This plugin must NOT wrap or proxy that class (per policy); it reimplements the formula directly.
SIGN CONVENTION — IGD is universally lower-is-better; no sign flip is needed. If PymooLab's plugin interface uses a 'higher-is-better' flag, it must be set to False.
DEGENERATE CASES — When S contains a single solution, IGD equals the mean distance from each PF point to that single solution; this is valid and must not raise an error.
spec_report.dimension_defaults_source_note: n_var_default=30 and n_obj_default=2 are taken verbatim from the PymooLab UI defaults/presets supplied in the user request. These values are consistent with standard benchmark usage (e.g., ZDT suite uses n_var=30, n_obj=2). They do NOT constrain the plugin; IGD is dimension-agnostic and works for any n_obj >= 2 and any n_var >= 1. The plugin derives n_obj at runtime from input array shapes.
spec_report._raw_text:
I'll search for trustworthy IGD implementations from official/academic sources before generating the specification.
All required signal has been gathered. I now have sufficient information from GitHub sources to synthesize the full specification. Key findings from the search:

-
IGD measures the average Euclidean distance in objective space from each solution in PF to the closest solution in S; the Pareto front as a whole needs to be covered by solutions from S to minimize the metric.
-
There is a known open question in the pymoo community about whether the parameter `p` in the IGD formula can be adjusted, such as `p=1`
— confirming pymoo's native IGD hardcodes `p=2` and does not expose a general p-norm.
-
The canonical IGD reference is Zhang, Qingfu, and Hui Li, "MOEA/D: A multiobjective evolutionary algorithm based on decomposition," IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation, 11.6 (2007): 712–731.
-
The standard averaging formula sums `distanceToClosestPoint` for each reference front point and divides by the number of reference front points.
-
jMetalPy implements quality indicators including hypervolume, additive epsilon, GD, IGD, and IGD+.

The plugin exposes `p` (float, default 2.0) as a user-tunable hyperparameter, accepting any p >= 1. Distances per reference point are computed via scipy.spatial or vectorised NumPy broadcasting over the full (|PF| x |S| x n_obj) tensor to avoid Python loops. payload.system: Act as a senior software engineer specialized in pymoo, PlatEMO, and optimization frameworks. Produce a compact specification JSON for code generation. Return strict JSON only.

messages.content:
Create a specification for generating a metric plugin for PymooLab.
Base name: igd_llm
PymooLab UI defaults/presets: n_var_default=30, n_obj_default=2
Request:
Implement the IGD indicator using the p-norm for an arbitrary number of objectives.

Metric-generation policy (GitHub-converted mode): use web_search in GitHub-only mode to recover trustworthy metric implementations/references (official/academic Python/MATLAB code) and preserve exact metric semantics when converting to PymooLab metric plugins. Do not propose canonical wrapper/proxy mode in this spec unless the user explicitly asks for it.

Return JSON with keys: summary (string), assumptions (list[string]), invariants (list[string]), limitations (list[string]), known_family_hint (string), metric_kind_hint (string), ambiguity_notes (list[string]), suggested_n_var_default (integer|null), suggested_n_obj_default (integer|null), dimension_defaults_source_note (string), task_kind (string), needs_external_sources (bool), fit_for_llm_agent (bool), unsupported_for_generation (bool).

intent_reasons: Prompt is compatible with single plugin generation workflow.

payload.system: Act as a senior software engineer specialized in pymoo, PlatEMO, and optimization frameworks. You are fixing invalid PymooLab metric plugin code. Return strict JSON with cpu_code and jax_code only.

messages.content:
Repair these two PymooLab metric modules for the original request:
Implement the IGD indicator using the p-norm for an arbitrary number of objectives.

Constraints: n_obj=2, base_name=IGD_LLM.
Keep create_metric(context) -> metric(front)->float, CPU/JAX parity, and PymooLab context compatibility.
Use web_search in GitHub-only mode to recover/re-check trustworthy metric implementations (official/academic Python/MATLAB repositories) and preserve metric semantics while converting to PymooLab.
Do not fall back to placeholder metric semantics/comments.
Do not repair by replacing the code with a trivial project canonical wrapper/proxy; preserve the requested metric semantics via GitHub-sourced implementations.
Fix the validation failures listed below.

Current CPU code:
# Made by PymooLab 2026.
# igd_llm.py
# PymooLab metric plugin: IGD with configurable p-norm (igd_llm)
#
# Formula (Convention A — arithmetic mean, jMetal/jMetalPy/MOEA-D standard):
#   IGD_p(S, PF) = (1/|PF|) * sum_{z in PF} min_{s in S} ||z - s||_p
#
# References:
#   - Zhang & Li, MOEA/D, IEEE TEC 11(6):712-731, 2007  (IGD canonical citation)
#   - Van Veldhuizen & Lamont, TR-98-03, 1998            (GD lineage)
#   - jMetal InvertedGenerationalDistance.java           (Java reference)
#   - jMetalPy quality_indicator.py                      (Python reference)
#   - Sierra & Coello Coello, 2004                       (p-norm generalisation)
#
# Outer aggregation: plain arithmetic mean over |PF| reference points.
# p-norm: used ONLY in the inner per-point Minkowski distance ||z-s||_p.
# No normalisation applied by default; caller is responsible.
# Does NOT wrap pymoo.indicators.igd.IGD (per policy).

import warnings
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.distance import cdist

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Core IGD computation (pure function, no side-effects)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _compute_igd_numpy(approx_front: np.ndarray,
                       ref_front: np.ndarray,
                       p: float) -> float:
    """
    Compute IGD_p(approx_front, ref_front) using scipy cdist for p==2 (fast
    Euclidean path) or vectorised NumPy broadcasting for general p.

    Parameters
    ----------
    approx_front : np.ndarray, shape (n_solutions, n_obj)
        Approximation front S in objective space.
    ref_front   : np.ndarray, shape (n_ref, n_obj)
        Reference (Pareto) front PF in objective space.
    p           : float
        Minkowski p-norm exponent (>= 1.0).

    Returns
    -------
    float
        IGD_p scalar in [0, +inf).  Lower is better.
    """
    # --- input validation ---
    approx_front = np.asarray(approx_front, dtype=np.float64)
    ref_front    = np.asarray(ref_front,    dtype=np.float64)

    if approx_front.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError(
            f"approx_front must be a 2-D array, got shape {approx_front.shape}")
    if ref_front.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError(
            f"ref_front must be a 2-D array, got shape {ref_front.shape}")
    if approx_front.shape[0] == 0:
        raise ValueError("approx_front (S) must be non-empty.")
    if ref_front.shape[0] == 0:
        raise ValueError("ref_front (PF) must be non-empty.")
    if approx_front.shape[1] != ref_front.shape[1]:
        raise ValueError(
            f"n_obj mismatch: approx_front has {approx_front.shape[1]} objectives "
            f"but ref_front has {ref_front.shape[1]}.")
    if p < 1.0:
        raise ValueError(f"p must be >= 1.0 to satisfy triangle inequality; got p={p}.")
    if p > 100.0:
        warnings.warn(
            f"p={p} > 100 may cause numerical instability in the p-norm; "
            "consider clamping p or using p=100 as a Chebyshev approximation.",
            RuntimeWarning, stacklevel=3)

    # --- pairwise distance matrix: shape (|PF|, |S|) ---
    # Use scipy cdist which internally dispatches to optimised BLAS routines.
    # cdist metric='minkowski' with p=2 is identical to 'euclidean' path.
    dist_matrix = cdist(ref_front, approx_front, metric='minkowski', p=p)
    # shape: (n_ref, n_solutions)

    # --- for each reference point: distance to closest solution ---
    min_dists = dist_matrix.min(axis=1)   # shape: (n_ref,)

    # --- outer aggregation: arithmetic mean (Convention A) ---
    igd_value = float(np.mean(min_dists))
    return igd_value


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Context helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _extract_context(context: dict) -> dict:
    """Support nested context['config'] as well as flat context dicts."""
    if context is None:
        return {}
    cfg = context.get('config', context)  # unwrap one level if nested
    return cfg if cfg is not None else {}


def _get_ref_front(cfg: dict) -> np.ndarray:
    """Resolve reference front from known PymooLab context keys."""
    for key in ('pareto_front', 'ref_pf', 'reference_front', 'pf'):
        pf = cfg.get(key)
        if pf is not None:
            return np.asarray(pf, dtype=np.float64)
    raise KeyError(
        "No reference front found in context. "
        "Expected one of: 'pareto_front', 'ref_pf', 'reference_front', 'pf'.")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plugin factory
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def create_metric(context: dict):
    """
    PymooLab metric plugin factory for IGD with configurable p-norm.

    Parameters read from context (or context['config']):
        p           : float, default 2.0  — Minkowski p-norm exponent (>= 1.0)
        pareto_front / ref_pf / reference_front / pf
                    : 2-D array-like — reference front PF

    Returns
    -------
    callable
        metric(front) -> float
        where `front` is the approximation front S as a 2-D array of shape
        (n_solutions, n_obj) in objective space.
    """
    cfg = _extract_context(context)

    # --- hyperparameter: p ---
    p = float(cfg.get('p', 2.0))
    if p < 1.0:
        raise ValueError(f"Hyperparameter p must be >= 1.0; got p={p}.")
    if p > 100.0:
        warnings.warn(
            f"Hyperparameter p={p} > 100 may cause numerical instability.",
            RuntimeWarning, stacklevel=2)

    # --- resolve and cache the reference front ---
    ref_front = _get_ref_front(cfg)
    if ref_front.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Reference front must be 2-D, got shape {ref_front.shape}.")
    if ref_front.shape[0] == 0:
        raise ValueError("Reference front (PF) must be non-empty.")

    # Freeze p as a local (stateless capture — no mutation between calls)
    _p = p
    _ref_front = ref_front

    def metric(front) -> float:
        """
        Compute IGD_p(front, PF).

        Parameters
        ----------
        front : array-like, shape (n_solutions, n_obj)
            Approximation front in objective space.

        Returns
        -------
        float
            IGD_p value in [0, +inf).  Lower is better (direction='minimize').
        """
        S = np.asarray(front, dtype=np.float64)
        if S.ndim == 1:
            # single solution edge-case: promote to (1, n_obj)
            S = S.reshape(1, -1)
        return _compute_igd_numpy(S, _ref_front, _p)

    # Attach metadata for PymooLab introspection
    metric.__name__       = 'igd_llm'
    metric.direction      = 'minimize'
    metric.lower_is_better = True
    metric.p              = _p
    metric.ref_front_shape = _ref_front.shape
    metric.__doc_extra__  = (
        f"IGD_p with p={_p}, ref_front shape={_ref_front.shape}. "
        "Formula: mean_{{z in PF}}(min_{{s in S}} ||z-s||_p). "
        "Convention A (arithmetic mean outer aggregation, jMetal/jMetalPy standard)."
    )
    return metric

Current JAX code:
# Made by PymooLab 2026.
# igd_llm_JAX.py
# PymooLab metric plugin: IGD with configurable p-norm — JAX variant (igd_llm_JAX)
#
# Formula (Convention A — arithmetic mean, jMetal/jMetalPy/MOEA-D standard):
#   IGD_p(S, PF) = (1/|PF|) * sum_{z in PF} min_{s in S} ||z - s||_p
#
# References:
#   - Zhang & Li, MOEA/D, IEEE TEC 11(6):712-731, 2007  (IGD canonical citation)
#   - Van Veldhuizen & Lamont, TR-98-03, 1998            (GD lineage)
#   - jMetal InvertedGenerationalDistance.java           (Java reference)
#   - jMetalPy quality_indicator.py                      (Python reference)
#   - Sierra & Coello Coello, 2004                       (p-norm generalisation)
#
# JAX strategy:
#   - Uses jax.numpy (jnp) for vectorised pairwise distance computation.
#   - Falls back to NumPy + scipy if JAX is not available.
#   - Numerically consistent with the CPU variant under float64 inputs.
#   - jax.jit compilation is optional; the returned metric callable is plain Python
#     (not jitted by default) to stay compatible with PymooLab's dispatch.
#
# Outer aggregation: plain arithmetic mean over |PF| reference points.
# p-norm: used ONLY in the inner per-point Minkowski distance ||z-s||_p.
# Does NOT wrap pymoo.indicators.igd.IGD (per policy).

import warnings
import numpy as np

try:
    import jax
    import jax.numpy as jnp
    # Enable 64-bit precision in JAX (critical for metric accuracy)
    jax.config.update('jax_enable_x64', True)
    _JAX_AVAILABLE = True
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover
    _JAX_AVAILABLE = False
    import numpy as jnp  # type: ignore[assignment]  # NumPy fallback alias

try:
    from scipy.spatial.distance import cdist as _scipy_cdist
    _SCIPY_AVAILABLE = True
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover
    _SCIPY_AVAILABLE = False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Core JAX computation (vectorised, no Python loops over points)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _compute_igd_jax(approx_front, ref_front, p: float) -> float:
    """
    Compute IGD_p using JAX-vectorised broadcasting.

    Pairwise distance tensor: (|PF|, |S|) computed via broadcasting over
    the (|PF|, |S|, n_obj) difference tensor — O(|PF|*|S|*n_obj) arithmetic.

    Parameters
    ----------
    approx_front : array-like, shape (n_solutions, n_obj)
    ref_front    : array-like, shape (n_ref, n_obj)
    p            : float, Minkowski exponent >= 1.0

    Returns
    -------
    float
    """
    if _JAX_AVAILABLE:
        S  = jnp.array(approx_front, dtype=jnp.float64)   # (n_s, n_obj)
        PF = jnp.array(ref_front,    dtype=jnp.float64)   # (n_r, n_obj)

        # Broadcasting: PF[:, None, :] - S[None, :, :] -> (n_r, n_s, n_obj)
        diff = PF[:, None, :] - S[None, :, :]              # (n_r, n_s, n_obj)

        if p == 2.0:
            # Euclidean: stable via squared sum then sqrt
            dist = jnp.sqrt(jnp.sum(diff ** 2, axis=-1))   # (n_r, n_s)
        elif p == 1.0:
            # Manhattan: sum of absolute values
            dist = jnp.sum(jnp.abs(diff), axis=-1)          # (n_r, n_s)
        else:
            # General Minkowski: sum(|d|^p)^(1/p)
            dist = jnp.sum(jnp.abs(diff) ** p, axis=-1) ** (1.0 / p)  # (n_r, n_s)

        min_dists = jnp.min(dist, axis=1)                   # (n_r,)
        igd_value = float(jnp.mean(min_dists))
    else:
        # Pure NumPy fallback (identical semantics, no JAX dependency)
        S  = np.asarray(approx_front, dtype=np.float64)
        PF = np.asarray(ref_front,    dtype=np.float64)
        diff = PF[:, None, :] - S[None, :, :]               # (n_r, n_s, n_obj)
        if p == 2.0:
            dist = np.sqrt(np.sum(diff ** 2, axis=-1))
        elif p == 1.0:
            dist = np.sum(np.abs(diff), axis=-1)
        else:
            dist = np.sum(np.abs(diff) ** p, axis=-1) ** (1.0 / p)
        min_dists = dist.min(axis=1)
        igd_value = float(np.mean(min_dists))

    return igd_value


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Context helper (mirrors CPU module)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _extract_context(context: dict) -> dict:
    """Support nested context['config'] as well as flat context dicts."""
    if context is None:
        return {}
    cfg = context.get('config', context)
    return cfg if cfg is not None else {}


def _get_ref_front(cfg: dict) -> np.ndarray:
    """Resolve reference front from known PymooLab context keys."""
    for key in ('pareto_front', 'ref_pf', 'reference_front', 'pf'):
        pf = cfg.get(key)
        if pf is not None:
            return np.asarray(pf, dtype=np.float64)
    raise KeyError(
        "No reference front found in context. "
        "Expected one of: 'pareto_front', 'ref_pf', 'reference_front', 'pf'.")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plugin factory
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def create_metric(context: dict):
    """
    PymooLab JAX metric plugin factory for IGD with configurable p-norm.

    Parameters read from context (or context['config']):
        p           : float, default 2.0  — Minkowski p-norm exponent (>= 1.0)
        pareto_front / ref_pf / reference_front / pf
                    : 2-D array-like — reference front PF

    Returns
    -------
    callable
        metric(front) -> float
        where `front` is the approximation front S as a 2-D array of shape
        (n_solutions, n_obj) in objective space.
    """
    cfg = _extract_context(context)

    # --- hyperparameter: p ---
    p = float(cfg.get('p', 2.0))
    if p < 1.0:
        raise ValueError(f"Hyperparameter p must be >= 1.0; got p={p}.")
    if p > 100.0:
        warnings.warn(
            f"Hyperparameter p={p} > 100 may cause numerical instability.",
            RuntimeWarning, stacklevel=2)

    # --- resolve and cache the reference front ---
    ref_front = _get_ref_front(cfg)
    if ref_front.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Reference front must be 2-D, got shape {ref_front.shape}.")
    if ref_front.shape[0] == 0:
        raise ValueError("Reference front (PF) must be non-empty.")

    # --- input shape validation against ref_front ---
    _p         = p
    _ref_front = ref_front

    def metric(front) -> float:
        """
        Compute IGD_p(front, PF) using JAX-vectorised broadcasting.

        Parameters
        ----------
        front : array-like, shape (n_solutions, n_obj)
            Approximation front in objective space.

        Returns
        -------
        float
            IGD_p value in [0, +inf).  Lower is better (direction='minimize').
        """
        S = np.asarray(front, dtype=np.float64)
        if S.ndim == 1:
            S = S.reshape(1, -1)
        if S.ndim != 2:
            raise ValueError(
                f"front must be a 1-D or 2-D array, got shape {S.shape}.")
        if S.shape[0] == 0:
            raise ValueError("approx_front (S) must be non-empty.")
        if S.shape[1] != _ref_front.shape[1]:
            raise ValueError(
                f"n_obj mismatch: front has {S.shape[1]} objectives "
                f"but ref_front has {_ref_front.shape[1]}.")
        return _compute_igd_jax(S, _ref_front, _p)

    # Attach metadata for PymooLab introspection
    metric.__name__        = 'igd_llm_JAX'
    metric.direction       = 'minimize'
    metric.lower_is_better = True
    metric.p               = _p
    metric.jax_available   = _JAX_AVAILABLE
    metric.ref_front_shape = _ref_front.shape
    metric.__doc_extra__   = (
        f"IGD_p (JAX) with p={_p}, ref_front shape={_ref_front.shape}. "
        "Formula: mean_{{z in PF}}(min_{{s in S}} ||z-s||_p). "
        "Convention A (arithmetic mean outer aggregation, jMetal/jMetalPy standard). "
        f"JAX backend: {'available' if _JAX_AVAILABLE else 'unavailable — NumPy fallback active'}."
    )
    return metric

Return JSON only with corrected cpu_code and jax_code.
